PDMR Dealing
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company, announces that
it
has been notified of a disclosable transaction carried out by one of its directors and persons discharging managerial responsibilities ("
PDMR
") on 18 March 2026.
The Company was notified by Mr. Christian Schaffalitzky de Muckadell, Non-Executive Director of the Company, that he has purchased 138,000 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each in the capital of the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
") at 7.75p per Ordinary Share. Following this purchase Mr. Schaffalitzky de Muckadell is interested in 258,999 Ordinary Shares, representing 0.67% of the Company's issued Ordinary Share capital.
The information set out below is provided in accordance with the requirements of Article 19(3) of the UK Market Abuse Regulation.

latest.title: Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 14:57
RNS Number : 1921X
Atlas Metals Group PLC
18 March 2026
18 March 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company announces that, further to the Company's establishment of an
"at-the-market" facility with Axis Capital Markets Limited ("
Axis
";
"
At-the-Market Facility
"
)
, application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 5,500,000 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
5,500,000 Ordinary Shares will be issued to Axis at par value, in connection with the At-the-Market Facility ("
ATM Shares
"). The ATM Shares are equal to approximately 14.27% of the Company's issued Ordinary Share capital following Admission. When issued, the ATM Shares will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company. As announced by the Company on 5 March 2026, further to the Company's entry into a funding facility with YA II PN Ltd, an institutional investor managed by Yorkville Advisors Global, LP, all proceeds from the
At-the-Market Facility will go towards repayment of this funding facility.
All Ordinary Shares previously issued pursuant to the At-the-Market Facility have been placed and Axis currently holds no Ordinary Shares.
Admission of the ATM Shares is expected to occur on or around 19 March 2026.
Total voting rights
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 38,532,930 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 38,532,930. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: Atlas Metals Group plc is a natural‑resources company that owns and develops a uranium‑mining asset in Kyrgyzstan. The firm also explores other base‑metal projects, but its core focus is on extracting and selling uranium for nuclear power and industrial use.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is a key feedstock for nuclear reactors worldwide, and demand is set to grow as many countries look to low‑carbon energy. Atlas Metals holds a unique, well‑located deposit that could fill a niche in the global supply chain, giving it a strategic advantage over smaller, less‑established producers.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in the early development phase—still building its operational base and securing financing. Recent moves include a funding facility, new share issues, and director purchases, all aimed at strengthening its balance sheet and moving toward production. The share price is trading very low, reflecting the high risk of a still‑unproven asset.

rns_narrative: The first thread is the company’s financing activity: a £2.5 million ATM facility, new ordinary shares, and a PDMR dealing that all signal an effort to shore up capital for development.
The second thread is asset development: updates on the Kyrgyz uranium project and a potential acquisition that could expand the company’s resource base.
The third thread is governance and confidence signals: several directors are buying shares, and the company is issuing new voting rights that suggest leadership believes the turnaround is coming.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bullish chatter around director buying and fresh funding
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Traders point to director purchases, a new funding line, and the promise of uranium production as a recipe for a big rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Skeptics warn that the company is still a penny stock with no revenue, high leverage, and regulatory uncertainty around mining approvals.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood is cautiously optimistic—few people are talking, but those who are say the company is on the verge of a turnaround.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, giving the company a surprise edge if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the £2.5 million ATM facility and a clear schedule for using those funds.
A formal announcement that the Kyrgyz uranium mine will start producing within the next 12–18 months.
A closing of the proposed acquisition that would add reserves or technology.
Any regulatory approval that removes a major hurdle for mining operations.
A spike in trading volume that signals renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a price move above the latest low, a sustained volume increase, and any official statement about production or acquisition progress. Those signals would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is deep in a crash—most of the selling has already happened. The APEX score indicates a tight range that could snap back once the catalysts materialise. Early capitulation is likely, so the next move will be a breakout from the low side.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company sits at a 70‑95 % drop, which historically delivers the biggest rebounds. Director buying shows internal confidence, and the new funding line gives the project a realistic path to production. If the share price climbs back to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside is roughly double the current level, while the downside is limited to the very low base price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a clear production timeline, the firm remains a penny stock with high leverage and regulatory risk. If the funding line fails or the mine never starts, the price could stay near zero or drop further. The worst case is a flat or slightly declining share price with no catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside >2× current price versus a maximum loss capped at the current low. The asymmetry is in favour of the trade, provided the catalysts arrive.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the latest low with a 10–15 % position of your capital. Place a stop 20 % below the entry to protect against a false move. Adjust the stop to breakeven once the price moves 10 % in your favour.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a confirmed funding announcement or a production‑related press release. Use a 0.5‑percentage‑point volume filter to confirm genuine interest before buying.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable—there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Atlas Metals sits at a deep crash, director buying and fresh funding are the catalysts, and the upside is large while the downside is capped, making it a contrarian bullish play.
